# Encypher - Content Intelligence Infrastructure
# https://encypher.com
# Last Updated: December 2025

## About Encypher

Encypher authored Section A.7 of the C2PA 2.3 specification (text provenance) and provides sentence-level content tracking infrastructure for publishers and AI labs.

### What We Do
- Authors of C2PA Section A.7 (text provenance, C2PA 2.3 specification)
- Sentence-level content authentication with cryptographic proof
- Court-admissible evidence generation for AI copyright litigation
- Content licensing infrastructure for the AI economy
- Performance intelligence for AI model optimization

### Who We Serve

**Publishers**: Transform unmarked content into provably owned assets. Generate court-admissible evidence for AI copyright litigation. Create new licensing revenue streams when AI companies use your content.

**AI Labs**: Get performance intelligence on your models across the internet. Track which parameters drive viral content. One integration covers the entire publisher ecosystem. EU AI Act and China watermarking mandate compliance.

**Enterprises**: Implement AI governance with sentence-level audit trails. EU AI Act compliance infrastructure. Competitive intelligence on AI content usage.

### Key Differentiators
- 100% accuracy (cryptographic proof vs 26% accuracy of probabilistic AI detection)
- Sentence-level granularity (not just document-level)
- Court-admissible evidence (mathematical certainty, not statistical inference)
- Standards authority (we authored Section A.7 of the C2PA specification)
- Two-sided market (serves both publishers and AI labs)

### Standards & Affiliations
- C2PA (Coalition for Content Provenance and Authenticity) - Section A.7 Authors and Text Provenance Task Force Co-Chairs
- CAI (Content Authenticity Initiative) - Members
- Working alongside Adobe, Microsoft, Google, OpenAI, BBC

## Products & Pricing

### For Publishers
- **Starter (Free)**: Unlimited C2PA signing, 2 API keys, 65% revenue share
- **Professional ($99/mo)**: Sentence-level tracking, 10 API keys, 70% revenue share
- **Business ($499/mo)**: Enterprise features, team collaboration, 75% revenue share
- **Enterprise (Custom)**: White-glove implementation, custom SLAs, 80% revenue share

### For AI Labs
- Performance intelligence dashboard
- Model parameter tracking
- Publisher ecosystem integration
- Regulatory compliance (EU AI Act, China mandate)

## Technical Overview

### C2PA Text Standard
The C2PA text standard defines how text content is cryptographically authenticated. Key features:
- Unicode variation selector encoding (invisible to humans)
- Ed25519 cryptographic signatures
- Sentence-level Merkle tree hashing
- Tamper-evident chain of custody

### API Capabilities
- `/sign` - Sign content with C2PA-compliant metadata
- `/verify` - Verify signed content and extract metadata
- `/merkle/encode` - Sentence-level Merkle tree encoding
- `/merkle/attribution` - Check content against registered sentences
- `/streaming/*` - Real-time content signing for LLM outputs

### SDKs Available
- Python SDK (PyPI)
- TypeScript SDK (npm)
- Go SDK
- Rust SDK

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: What is Encypher?
A: Encypher authored Section A.7 of the C2PA 2.3 specification (text provenance) and provides sentence-level content tracking infrastructure for publishers and AI labs. We enable litigation evidence and content licensing through cryptographic proof with 100% accuracy.

Q: What is the C2PA text standard?
A: The C2PA specification, whose text provenance section (A.7) was authored by Encypher, defines how text content is cryptographically authenticated. It enables verification of content origin, modifications, and usage with mathematical certainty.

Q: How is Encypher different from AI detection tools?
A: AI detection tools provide statistical guessing with ~26% accuracy and high false positive rates. Encypher provides cryptographic proof with 100% accuracy and sentence-level granularity. Our evidence is court-admissible.

Q: What is sentence-level tracking?
A: Sentence-level tracking identifies and authenticates content at individual sentence granularity. Instead of proving "a document was accessed," we prove "sentences 47, 103, and 289 from this specific article were used."

Q: Who uses Encypher?
A: Publishers seeking court-admissible evidence for AI copyright litigation and content licensing revenue. AI labs requiring publisher ecosystem integration, model performance intelligence, and compliance infrastructure.

Q: Is Encypher an AI detection tool?
A: No. Encypher is infrastructure that enables content authentication. Once AI companies adopt our technology, detection becomes trivial because content carries cryptographic proof of origin. We're building the infrastructure layer, not a detection product.

Q: What regulations does Encypher help with?
A: EU AI Act (requires AI-generated content identification), China watermarking mandate, and general AI governance requirements. Our C2PA-compliant infrastructure satisfies these regulatory requirements.
